Advisor Guide
Welcome

Create Your Advisor Profile

The goal of your profile is to build immediate trust with the client by presenting an authentic, credible, and approachable advisor who offers genuine spiritual guidance. This guide will help you create a profile step by step, ensuring it meets Nebula's brand requirements and is clear and comfortable for clients.

In this guide, you will find out how to:
  • Write a slogan and profile bio with a clear structure and the right tone of voice.
  • Make a photo that looks professional and matches the style of the platform.
  • Record a profile video that establishes authenticity and credibility immediately.
Guide categories
Section 01

Profile & Bio

Your Bio is what establishes a personal connection with your future clients. The bio works best when it feels authentic, supportive, and well-structured.

Platform Nickname

On Nebula, advisors are identified by a platform nickname — it appears on the profile and in sessions.

ℹ NoteThe nickname must be unique to Nebula — not used on other platforms or social media.

ExamplesMiss Paris · Silver Luna · Aria K. · James · Moon Seeker

Your Slogan

The slogan sits just below your name and works best as one personal sentence that immediately conveys your voice — something a client reads and thinks: this person gets it.

Example"You are stronger than you think, and your next step is already waiting for you to see it."
Example"Every answer you seek already lives within you — I am here to help you find it."

Slogans on Nebula are in sentence case. One well-chosen sentence tends to be more memorable than two.

Tone and Voice

Bios on Nebula speak directly to the client — using "you" and "your" makes the text feel personal rather than distant.

Works well"If you are feeling lost, I am here to help you rediscover your path."
Less effective"Clients often come to me when they are feeling lost, and I help them rediscover their path."
  • Be genuine. Share why you do this work — a personal truth or philosophy reads better than generic or corporate phrasing.
  • Stay on-brand. Use "reading" rather than "consultation." Avoid words like "clairvoyant" or "magic" — they imply guaranteed outcomes or professional services.
  • Explain spiritual terms. If "archetypes" or "soul codes" come up, a brief clarification keeps the bio approachable.
Bios open by speaking directly to the client — not with "Hello, dear friend" or "Welcome to my sacred space." This keeps the tone consistent with Nebula: Spiritual Space.

Content

It helps to include specific details about your approach — what you feel, value, or believe in, and what drew you to your practice. Establishing a personal connection first, with any self-promotion coming later, tends to read best.

If you want to use spirituality-related terms (e.g. "archetypes," "soul codes"), it's best to provide a brief explanation or context so the client understands their meaning and feels more curious.

⚠ RequiredAll profile information needs to be accurate and verifiable. Advisors don't make false or misleading claims about experience, results, or residence.

Style

Break the bio into 2–5 paragraphs for readability. Use standard capitalization and avoid emojis. Use an em dash (—) or en dash (–) rather than hyphens as dashes.

Use "Reading" rather than "Consultation" in your bio — it feels warmer and more in line with the platform tone.

Bio Structure

Organizing your bio into these three distinct sections works best to keep clients engaged.

Paragraph I · Invitation

How do you describe the core feeling or purpose you bring to a reading?

Example"When you seek guidance, what is the most important thing you need to hear from me?"
Example"Are you searching for clarity and a deeper understanding of the choices before you? I believe that every answer you seek already lives within you, and my purpose is simply to illuminate that path for your own eyes."

Paragraph II · Expertise

What expertise (Tarot, Astrology, etc.) do you use, and how does it personally align with your life philosophy? (Explain the "why," not just the "what.")

Example"I use Tarot because it allows me to translate the universal language of energy into practical steps for your life."
Example"My approach is grounded in empathy and practical guidance. I specialize in [Key Expertise 1] and [Key Expertise 2]. For me, [Personal Philosophy / Belief], and through our reading, I connect with your energy to help you navigate challenges like [Common Pain Point 1] and [Common Pain Point 2]."

If you use a complex term (e.g. soul codes), it's recommended to explain its benefit simply to the reader.

Example"We will look at your 'soul codes' — the unique blueprint of your inner potential — to show you exactly where your energy is best invested right now."

Paragraph III · Closing Invitation

Closing the bio works best as a warm, direct invitation rather than a promise of results. On Nebula's platform, a reading offers a perspective and a space to reflect — the final choices and outcomes stay with the user. Framing the closing paragraph around the experience you offer, rather than a guaranteed outcome, keeps the bio on-brand.

Less effective"You won't just leave with predictions; you'll leave with actionable steps and a restored sense of inner peace." — reads as a guaranteed outcome.
Works well"Together we can look at what's weighing on you and explore a fresh perspective on the path ahead."

How would you invite the reader to take the next step? A closing that opens a door tends to land better.

Example"It takes courage to seek spiritual guidance, and I honor your journey. Let's start a reading together to explore your situation from a new angle and reflect on the way forward."
Next
Your Profile Photo
→
Section 02

Your Profile Photo

Your profile photo is the first thing clients see before reading your bio. It works best when it conveys a simple message: this psychic can be trusted with important life issues. The points below help a photo meet Nebula's standards.

⚠ RequiredAll photos must be real. AI-generated images are not accepted.

⚠ ImportantThe profile photo must be exclusive to Nebula — not published on other platforms or social media.

Compliance. Photos follow a few guidelines (background, ratio, color) to look professional and grounded.

Photo Quality Standards

Even a good-looking photo can be rejected if it doesn't meet basic technical quality.

Resolution & sharpness

  • High-resolution image
  • Face is sharp and clearly visible
  • No pixelation, grain, or compression artifacts
  • Stays clear after circular avatar crop

Lighting & color

  • Soft, even lighting (no harsh shadows)
  • No strong backlight or overexposure
  • Natural skin tones and correct white balance (no strong color cast)
  • No dramatic or cinematic color grading

Filters & editing

  • No beauty filters or face-smoothing
  • Retouching is minimal and natural (skin texture stays visible)
  • No artificial blur on facial features

Focus & exposure

  • Face in focus, no motion blur
  • Balanced exposure and contrast (no blown highlights or crushed blacks)

Cropping & framing

  • Face + hair fully visible
  • Head, chin, and forehead stay within frame
  • Face size looks natural (not too small or too close)

What makes a strong photo

Five principles — each captures what works and what to avoid.

Real & authentic

✓A real, unedited photo of yourself — natural skin texture, minimal retouching, genuine expression
✕AI-generated images, stock photos, or photos of another person are not accepted

Technical quality

✓Sharp, well-lit, high-resolution — face clearly visible in a circular crop
✕Passport-style photos, heavy filters, visible compression, overexposed or underlit shots

Framing & expression

✓Front-facing, face and hair fully visible, direct eye contact, calm and warm expression
✕Cropped forehead or chin, extreme angles, closed eyes, face covered with hands, props, or accessories

Background & setting

✓Neutral, clean background — plain wall or simple interior; light blur if needed
✕Party, wedding, bedroom, or random event setups; cluttered or distracting environments

Clothing & overall tone

✓Neutral or casual-professional clothing that does not distract from the face — a look that feels grounded, warm, and trustworthy
✕Heavy branding, logos, costumes, or props; sexualized imagery, revealing outfits, or any content that breaks the calm, trust-based tone of the platform

Examples of appropriate photos

Examples of inappropriate photos

Next
Video Guidelines
→
Section 03

Video Guidelines for Nebula Advisors

On Nebula, a video is part of every advisor profile. It gives clients a chance to hear your voice and get a feel for who you are before starting a reading.

What Works Well in a Nebula Video

  • Your nickname as shown on your Nebula profile
  • A natural human voice. AI-generated or synthetic voices fall outside Nebula's authenticity standards — clients come for a real human connection.
  • Your introduction: who you are, your background, and main skills
  • Engaging communication: eye contact, natural speech, warmth and confidence
  • Videos are in English only (Latin alphabet).
  • Simple, clean background: neutral, natural, or aesthetically pleasant (no visual clutter)
  • Correct terminology: On Nebula, the correct term to use in the video is "Nebula psychic" rather than advisor, astrologer, or other titles.
  • Positive tone: friendly and encouraging, even when covering complex topics

Better to Avoid

  • Watermarks from video editing or subtitle apps
  • Visible timestamps or dates on screen
  • References to other apps, social media, or personal contact details
  • Profanity, inappropriate language, nudity / sexual content, or alcohol / tobacco visible on screen
  • Background noise — street noise, TV, or conversations in the background tend to reduce video quality.
  • Poor video quality: pixelated, too dark, or overexposed footage

Technical Requirements

ParameterRequirement
OrientationVertical (portrait)
DurationUp to 60 seconds
File formatMP4, up to 20 MB
CameraSmartphone or camera (front or rear)
StabilityTripod or stable surface — no camera shake
LightingNatural light (window / outdoors) or ring light; avoid harsh shadows and glare
SoundQuiet room or outdoor quiet spot; do a sound test before recording
QualityGood resolution, well-lit, clear audio

Video Types — Choose One

Each type is self-contained. Pick the one that fits your style and follow the same intro + expertise + call to action structure.

Mystical

Introduce yourself while interacting with your tools — cards, crystals, or candles. A great choice if your work is visually grounded in spiritual props.

Natural background

Record outdoors and speak about your connection with nature. Works well if your practice is tied to the energy of natural environments.

Astrological

Introduce yourself and demonstrate your astrology expertise — walk through a natal chart or explain a current astrological transit.

Personal story

Face-to-camera, no props. Share your background, gifts, and what brought you to this work. Best for advisors whose strength is personal connection.

Crystal or tarot demonstration

Show your tools in action — demonstrate a crystal's properties or do a live tarot draw. Lets clients see your skills directly.

Final Checklist

Good to go

  • Vertical format, up to 60 seconds
  • Face fully visible, stable image
  • Eye contact with camera, natural expression
  • English only, "Nebula psychic" terminology used
  • No watermarks or timestamps
  • Clear audio, no background noise
  • File is MP4, up to 20 MB

Common issues

  • Cluttered or distracting background
  • Background noise (TV, street, conversations)
  • Reading from a script
  • References to other apps or personal contact details
  • Electronic watch showing time or language

Before opening the form, make sure your photo, video, bio, and slogan are ready.
